chunks.title: Introduction and Setting
chunks.reason: Moses introduces the location, time, and purpose of his address to Israel.

chunks.title: Recall of Horeb and Judicial Reform
chunks.reason: Moses recounts God's command to enter the land and his appointment of leaders to assist in judgment.

chunks.title: The Rebellion at Kadesh-barnea
chunks.reason: A narrative account of the scouts' mission, the people's fear, and God's judgment on that generation.

chunks.title: Presumptuous Entry and Defeat
chunks.reason: The people attempt to enter the land despite God's warning, suffer defeat, and settle in Kadesh.

chunks.title: Journey through Seir and Moab
chunks.reason: Narrative of the Israelites circling Mount Seir and entering Moabite territory.

chunks.title: Instructions for Moab and Zered Crossing
chunks.reason: Divine commands regarding Moab combined with the narrative of crossing the brook Zered.

chunks.title: Transition to Ammonite Territory
chunks.reason: Completion of the forty-year wandering and historical notes on the land of Ammon.

chunks.title: Conquest of Sihon King of Heshbon
chunks.reason: Divine command to take land from Sihon, the diplomatic attempt, and the subsequent military victory.

chunks.title: Conquest of Bashan and Land Distribution
chunks.reason: Narrative account of the battle with Og and the allocation of territory to Reuben, Gad, and Manasseh.

chunks.title: Command to the Transjordan Tribes
chunks.reason: Moses instructs the tribes on their duty to cross the Jordan while their families remain behind.

chunks.title: Encouragement to Joshua
chunks.reason: Moses speaks directly to Joshua about God's power and the upcoming conquest.

chunks.title: Moses' Plea and Final Commission
chunks.reason: Combines Moses' prayer to enter the land, God's response, and the final instruction to Joshua.

chunks.title: Moses' Exhortation to Obey the Law
chunks.reason: A continuous speech where Moses urges Israel to keep the statutes, recalls the Horeb covenant, warns against idolatry, and affirms God's uniqueness.

chunks.title: Appointment of Cities of Refuge
chunks.reason: A narrative account of Moses designating three cities for the manslayer.

chunks.title: Summary of the Law and Geography
chunks.reason: A concluding list summarizing the law given to Israel and cataloging the geographical boundaries of their territory.

chunks.title: Moses Summons Israel
chunks.reason: Moses introduces the covenant and context of the law.

chunks.title: The Ten Commandments
chunks.reason: Direct speech containing the core laws given by God.

chunks.title: People's Reaction to the Voice
chunks.reason: Narration of the people's fear and request for Moses as mediator.

chunks.title: God's Response to the People
chunks.reason: God acknowledges their fear and instructs Moses to teach them.

chunks.title: Call to Obedience
chunks.reason: Final charge to the people to follow the commandments carefully.

chunks.title: The Great Commandment and Teaching
chunks.reason: A unified call to hear, love God, and teach His words diligently.

chunks.title: Warnings Upon Entering the Land
chunks.reason: Instructions on how to live in the promised land without forgetting God.

chunks.title: Instruction for Future Generations
chunks.reason: A dialogue between a son and father explaining the statutes.

chunks.title: Instructions on Conquering Nations
chunks.reason: Establishes the command to destroy seven nations and avoid intermarriage.

chunks.title: Reason for God's Choice
chunks.reason: Explains that Israel was chosen due to God's love and covenant rather than size.

chunks.title: Promises of Blessing for Obedience
chunks.reason: Details the rewards of keeping God's judgments including prosperity and health.

chunks.title: Encouragement to Trust in Battle
chunks.reason: Addresses potential fear of numerous enemies and commands total destruction of idols.

chunks.title: Remember the Wilderness Journey
chunks.reason: Moses exhorts Israel to remember God's discipline and testing in the wilderness as a model for their current obedience.

chunks.title: Description of the Promised Land
chunks.reason: A descriptive passage detailing the abundance of the land and the command to bless God upon entering it.

chunks.title: Warning Against Pride in Wealth
chunks.reason: An argument warning that prosperity may lead to forgetting God and attributing wealth to one's own power.

chunks.title: Consequence of Idolatry
chunks.reason: A concluding oracle declaring that forgetting God and serving other gods will result in perishing like the nations before them.

chunks.title: Warning Against Pride
chunks.reason: Moses exhorts Israel to remember God's power and their own lack of righteousness.

chunks.title: The Golden Calf Incident
chunks.reason: Moses recounts the history of Israel's rebellion at Horeb and his intercession.

chunks.title: Summary of Rebellions
chunks.reason: A brief list of previous locations where Israel provoked God.

chunks.title: Moses' Final Intercession
chunks.reason: Moses pleads with God to spare the people based on His covenant and reputation.

chunks.title: The Second Tablets and Ark
chunks.reason: Narrative account of Moses preparing the second set of tablets and the ark.

chunks.title: Israel's Journey and Levitical Priesthood
chunks.reason: Historical narrative detailing Israel's travel route and the specific role of the tribe of Levi.

chunks.title: Moses' Second Stay on the Mountain
chunks.reason: Continuation of the narrative regarding Moses' intercession and God's response.

chunks.title: Call to Covenant Faithfulness
chunks.reason: A unified speech urging Israel to fear God, keep His commandments, and remember His love.

chunks.title: Call to Love and Remember
chunks.reason: Moses exhorts Israel to love God and remember His past disciplines.

chunks.title: Land Comparison and Warning
chunks.reason: Contrasts the new land with Egypt and warns of consequences for disobedience.

chunks.title: Instructions on Obedience
chunks.reason: Details the practice of binding God's words and the resulting blessings.

chunks.title: Blessing and Curse
chunks.reason: Sets forth the choice between blessing and curse with specific instructions for Mount Gerizim and Ebal.

chunks.title: Centralization of Worship
chunks.reason: Establishes the command to destroy local shrines and worship only at the chosen place.

chunks.title: Regulations on Meat and Tithes
chunks.reason: Distinguishes between eating meat locally versus bringing tithes to the central sanctuary.

chunks.title: Warning Against Idolatry
chunks.reason: Admonishes the people to avoid adopting the religious practices of the conquered nations.

chunks.title: Final Charge
chunks.reason: Concludes the chapter with a summary command to observe the statutes precisely.

chunks.title: Law on False Prophets
chunks.reason: Establishes the penalty for a prophet who leads Israel to other gods despite performing signs.

chunks.title: Law on Family Enticement
chunks.reason: Details the strict judgment required when close relatives secretly entice others to idolatry.

chunks.title: Law on Wicked Cities
chunks.reason: Describes the procedure for investigating and destroying a whole city that has turned to idolatry.

chunks.title: Identity and Holiness
chunks.reason: Establishes the people's status as God's treasured possession.

chunks.title: Dietary Laws
chunks.reason: Covers clean and unclean animals, birds, and water creatures.

chunks.title: Tithing Instructions
chunks.reason: Details the annual tithe, consumption at the sanctuary, and provisions for distance.

chunks.title: Triennial Tithe
chunks.reason: Describes the third-year tithe for the Levite and poor.

chunks.title: Laws on Debt Remission
chunks.reason: A cohesive legal unit detailing the seven-year debt release and the attitude required toward the poor.

chunks.title: Laws on Hebrew Servants
chunks.reason: A distinct legal section governing the release of Hebrew servants and their provision upon freedom.

chunks.title: Laws on Firstborn Animals
chunks.reason: A complete set of regulations concerning the consecration and consumption of firstborn livestock.

chunks.title: Instructions for Passover and Unleavened Bread
chunks.reason: A continuous legal section detailing the observance of the first feast.

chunks.title: Instructions for the Feast of Weeks
chunks.reason: A distinct legal unit covering the timing and celebration of the second feast.

chunks.title: Instructions for the Feast of Booths and Pilgrimage
chunks.reason: A legal section describing the third feast and the requirement for males to appear before God.

chunks.title: Appointment of Judges and Officers
chunks.reason: A specific legal directive regarding the establishment of civil justice.

chunks.title: Prohibitions Against Idolatry
chunks.reason: A concluding legal unit forbidding specific pagan symbols near the altar.

chunks.title: Laws on Sacrifices and Idolatry
chunks.reason: Establishes regulations for sacrificial animals and the judicial process for idolaters.

chunks.title: Laws on Judicial Appeals
chunks.reason: Describes the procedure for difficult cases and the authority of Levitical priests.

chunks.title: Laws on the King
chunks.reason: Outlines qualifications and restrictions for the future king of Israel.

chunks.title: Priestly Inheritance and Portions
chunks.reason: Defines the legal status of Levitical priests regarding inheritance and specific offerings.

chunks.title: Warning Against Abominations
chunks.reason: Commands the people to avoid pagan practices and remain blameless before God.

chunks.title: The Prophet Like Moses
chunks.reason: Prophesies the coming of a prophet and establishes criteria for testing his authenticity.

chunks.title: Cities of Refuge and Manslayer Regulations
chunks.reason: Establishes the command to set apart cities for those who kill without premeditation and distinguishes them from intentional murderers.

chunks.title: Boundary Marks and Judicial Procedure
chunks.reason: Contains instructions regarding property boundaries, the requirement of multiple witnesses, and the principle of proportional justice.

chunks.title: Exhortations Before Battle
chunks.reason: A continuous speech by the priest and officers encouraging the troops and granting exemptions.

chunks.title: Rules for Conquering Cities
chunks.reason: Legal instructions distinguishing between distant cities and local Canaanite nations regarding peace terms and destruction.

chunks.title: Rules for Siege Warfare
chunks.reason: Specific regulations concerning the treatment of trees during a prolonged siege.

chunks.title: Ritual for Unidentified Slain Person
chunks.reason: Describes the legal procedure and ritual cleansing required when a body is found in an open field.

chunks.title: Law Regarding Captive Wife
chunks.reason: Outlines the process for a soldier taking a foreign woman as a wife and her subsequent status.

chunks.title: Rights of the Firstborn Son
chunks.reason: Establishes inheritance rights for the firstborn son even if born to an unloved wife.

chunks.title: The Stubborn and Rebellious Son
chunks.reason: Details the judicial process for a son who refuses parental discipline.

chunks.title: Burial of the Hanged Man
chunks.reason: Commands the timely burial of a executed criminal to prevent land defilement.

chunks.title: Lost Property and Fallen Beasts
chunks.reason: Laws regarding the return of lost livestock and helping fallen animals.

chunks.title: Various Statutes on Clothing and Agriculture
chunks.reason: Laws concerning mixed garments, gender-specific clothing, bird nests, roof parapets, and agricultural mixtures.

chunks.title: Cases of Virginity Disputes
chunks.reason: Legal procedures for a husband accusing his wife of non-virginity and the consequences if true or false.

chunks.title: Adultery and Fiancee Cases
chunks.reason: Laws regarding adultery with a married woman and the distinction between forced and voluntary relations with an engaged virgin.

chunks.title: Unmarried Virgin and Father's Wife
chunks.reason: Laws concerning a man seizing an unmarried virgin and the prohibition against taking a father's wife.

chunks.title: Restrictions on Assembly Entry
chunks.reason: A cohesive set of laws defining who may enter the assembly based on physical condition, lineage, and nationality.

chunks.title: Camp Purity Regulations
chunks.reason: Instructions regarding ritual cleanliness for soldiers during military campaigns.

chunks.title: Social and Economic Laws
chunks.reason: A collection of statutes covering escaped slaves, cultic prostitution, interest rates, vows, and field rights.

chunks.title: Law on Remarriage after Divorce
chunks.reason: A complete legal case regarding a woman divorced twice.

chunks.title: Laws on Newlyweds and Leapers
chunks.reason: Distinct legal statutes regarding new wives, millstones, and leprosy.

chunks.title: Laws on Loans and Wages
chunks.reason: A coherent unit covering the treatment of debtors and hired workers.

chunks.title: Laws on Justice and Harvest
chunks.reason: Legal instructions on judicial accountability, social justice, and agricultural charity.

chunks.title: Judicial Stripes
chunks.reason: Establishes the limit of forty stripes for a condemned man to prevent dishonor.

chunks.title: Ox and Levirate Marriage
chunks.reason: Combines the law regarding an ox threshing with the duty of a brother to marry his deceased sibling's widow.

chunks.title: The Sandal Ceremony
chunks.reason: Describes the specific ritual of removing a sandal and spitting when a brother refuses the levirate duty.

chunks.title: The Woman's Hand
chunks.reason: Prescribes cutting off a woman's hand who seizes a man's genitals during a struggle.

chunks.title: Just Weights and Measures
chunks.reason: Commands the use of fair weights and measures to avoid being an abomination to God.

chunks.title: Blotting Out Amalek
chunks.reason: Reminds Israel of Amalek's attack and commands the total destruction of their memory.

chunks.title: Firstfruits Offering Ritual
chunks.reason: Describes the procedure for bringing firstfruits and includes the declaration of faith.

chunks.title: Third Year Tithe Declaration
chunks.reason: Details the distribution of tithes to the needy and the accompanying confession.

chunks.title: Covenant Commitment
chunks.reason: Summarizes the mutual commitment between God and Israel regarding statutes and blessings.

chunks.title: Instructions for the Stones and Altar
chunks.reason: Moses commands the people to set up stones with the law and build an altar on Mount Ebal.

chunks.title: Call to Listen and Obey
chunks.reason: Moses and the priests address Israel to become a people for Yahweh and listen to His voice.

chunks.title: Placement of Tribes for Blessing and Curse
chunks.reason: Moses instructs which tribes stand on Gerizim to bless and which on Ebal to curse.

chunks.title: The Twelve Curses
chunks.reason: A series of twelve specific curses pronounced by the Levites with the people's response.

chunks.title: Blessings for Obedience
chunks.reason: A cohesive unit describing the comprehensive blessings promised to Israel for keeping God's commandments.

chunks.title: The Curses of Disobedience
chunks.reason: A detailed enumeration of specific curses and calamities that will befall the people if they fail to listen to God.

chunks.title: Consequences and Exile
chunks.reason: A concluding section explaining the cause of the curses, detailing the siege, and describing the final exile to Egypt.

chunks.title: Introduction to the Covenant in Moab
chunks.reason: Moses summarizes past deliverance and commands obedience to the new covenant.

chunks.title: The Covenant Ceremony and Warning
chunks.reason: Moses addresses the assembly, defines the scope of the covenant, warns against apostasy, and concludes with a statement on revealed truth.

chunks.title: Promise of Restoration and Blessing
chunks.reason: Describes the conditions for return from exile and the resulting divine blessings.

chunks.title: The Accessibility of the Law
chunks.reason: Explains that God's command is near and not distant.

chunks.title: Choice Between Life and Death
chunks.reason: Presents the final choice to the people with a call to choose life.

chunks.title: Moses Addresses Israel
chunks.reason: Moses announces his age and the Lord's command regarding the Jordan.

chunks.title: Charge to Joshua
chunks.reason: Moses encourages Joshua before all Israel to lead the people.

chunks.title: Reading the Law Every Seven Years
chunks.reason: Moses instructs the priests and elders on the periodic public reading of the law.

chunks.title: Commissioning at the Tent of Meeting
chunks.reason: Narrative of Moses and Joshua appearing before the Lord to commission Joshua.

chunks.title: Moses' Final Charge to Elders
chunks.reason: Moses commands the Levites and gathers elders to witness his final words.

chunks.title: Singing the Song
chunks.reason: Moses recites the song to the assembly of Israel.

chunks.title: Song of Moses
chunks.reason: A unified poetic speech containing an oracle, argument, and blessing.

chunks.title: Moses Delivers the Song
chunks.reason: Narrative account of Moses presenting the song and giving final exhortations.

chunks.title: Moses' Death Foretold
chunks.reason: Divine instruction to Moses regarding his death and view of the Promised Land.

chunks.title: Introduction to the Blessing
chunks.reason: Sets the context for Moses' final blessing before his death.

chunks.title: Blessings of the Tribes
chunks.reason: Contains individual blessings for each tribe followed by a concluding general blessing.

chunks.title: Moses Sees the Promised Land
chunks.reason: Describes Moses viewing the land and God's declaration that he will not enter it.

chunks.title: Death and Burial of Moses
chunks.reason: Narrates the death, burial, age, and mourning period for Moses.

chunks.title: Succession of Joshua and Praise of Moses
chunks.reason: Details Joshua's appointment and concludes with a summary of Moses' unique prophetic status.
